feature_surface_analysis.installed_4_21_evidence: Installed Windows app build on 2026-04-21 and generated config keeps remote_connections enabled.
feature_surface_analysis.official_reference_summary: OpenAI remote connections docs require remote_connections=true and require codex on the remote login-shell PATH.
feature_surface_analysis.how_it_affects_dev_management: Codex App is the main UI for opening devmgmt-wsl remote projects while Dev-Management remains authority.
feature_surface_analysis.guard_impact: Windows-side SSH alias and remote codex PATH must be verified.
feature_surface_analysis.audit_impact: Remote readiness and remote codex PATH are audit sections.
feature_surface_analysis.repair_impact: User-level Windows SSH config repair is allowed; system SSH and portproxy remain manual.
feature_surface_analysis.test_impact: Windows app-side SSH readiness and remote codex classification tests are required.

feature_surface_analysis.installed_4_21_evidence: Current app model supports Windows UI with Linux execution.
feature_surface_analysis.official_reference_summary: OpenAI Windows docs describe Windows-native and WSL agents as separate execution choices.
feature_surface_analysis.how_it_affects_dev_management: Windows host stays app_host/ssh_client_surface and devmgmt-wsl stays canonical_remote_execution_surface.
feature_surface_analysis.guard_impact: App cannot redefine execution or policy authority.
feature_surface_analysis.repair_impact: Docs/contracts/AGENTS encode the split; no binary modification.
feature_surface_analysis.test_impact: Codex App not execution authority tests remain required.

feature_surface_analysis.installed_4_21_evidence: Terminal surface exists separately from the selected agent surface.
feature_surface_analysis.official_reference_summary: OpenAI Windows docs note the integrated terminal is independent from the agent configuration.
feature_surface_analysis.how_it_affects_dev_management: Terminal-specific PATH drift becomes evidence, not default authority.
feature_surface_analysis.guard_impact: Selected execution terminal with contaminated PATH is blocked only when used as execution authority.
feature_surface_analysis.audit_impact: Terminal mismatch and PATH contamination are warn-or-block evidence.
feature_surface_analysis.repair_impact: Report-only unless authority-managed helpers are in scope.

feature_surface_analysis.installed_4_21_evidence: Current session exposes the GitHub plugin, MCP servers, skills, and subagents.
feature_surface_analysis.official_reference_summary: OpenAI plugin docs describe plugins as bundles of skills, apps, and MCP servers that do not bypass approvals.
feature_surface_analysis.how_it_affects_dev_management: Plugins and MCP are toolchain surfaces with provenance, not authority sources.
feature_surface_analysis.guard_impact: Plugin MCP drift affecting Serena or Context7 must be audited before code modification.
feature_surface_analysis.audit_impact: Toolchain surface report records plugin, MCP, skill, and subagent provenance.
feature_surface_analysis.repair_impact: Report-only unless authority-managed config is in scope.
feature_surface_analysis.test_impact: plugin MCP and provenance tests remain required.

feature_surface_analysis.installed_4_21_evidence: Chronicle is enabled; app memory and projectless chat state exist as runtime evidence.
feature_surface_analysis.official_reference_summary: OpenAI docs treat memories and related app state as context surfaces.
feature_surface_analysis.how_it_affects_dev_management: App memory is a hint only and projectless code modification without repo root stays blocked.
feature_surface_analysis.guard_impact: Chronicle allowed; telepathy forbidden; app memory cannot authorize code changes.
feature_surface_analysis.audit_impact: Stale inactive app state is WARN evidence only.
feature_surface_analysis.repair_impact: No automatic repair beyond drift reporting.
feature_surface_analysis.test_impact: projectless-chat and memory-not-authority tests remain required.

feature_surface_analysis.feature: automations / worktrees / local environments / browser / computer use / settings sync / app server
feature_surface_analysis.installed_4_21_evidence: These app surfaces exist and can influence local runtime state or review flow.
feature_surface_analysis.official_reference_summary: OpenAI app feature pages list automations, worktrees, local environments, browser, and related UI surfaces.
feature_surface_analysis.how_it_affects_dev_management: They are audit surfaces but cannot replace authority or canonical runtime.
feature_surface_analysis.guard_impact: App update or settings sync cannot reintroduce Windows launcher as primary runtime.
feature_surface_analysis.audit_impact: Surface presence is recorded in release-impact and toolchain reports.
feature_surface_analysis.repair_impact: Mostly report-only; authority-managed generated surfaces may be rerendered.
feature_surface_analysis.test_impact: release-driven surface coverage tests remain required.

operational_conclusion.blocked_relation: Codex App on Windows -> /mnt/c/Users/anise/.codex/bin/wsl/codex -> primary execution runtime

hooks.notes: Hooks remain trigger-only; audit, tests, and score layer are the final gates.

notes: Installed app evidence and remote-connection behavior were treated as evidence, not authority.
Linux-native Codex CLI on devmgmt-wsl is the canonical agent binary target for app usability.
